growth_records.medium: riboflavin fermentation medium (composition in Supplementary Method 1; not in full text)

sec.method: HPLC (peak area at 370 nm); 5-L fed-batch bioreactor
derived: 5.03 g/L / 376.36 g/mol = 13.36 mmol/L; /28 h = 0.477 mmol/L/h

growth_records.cite: Zhiheng Yang et al. A thermostable type I-B CRISPR-Cas system for orthogonal and multiplexed genetic engineering. Nature Communications (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41467-023-41973-5. [PMC10551041]
growth_records.snippet: Finally, in a 5-L bioreactor, the performance of the engineered Gt-09 strain produced riboflavin with a final titer of 5.03 g/L within 28 h ... which represents the highest riboflavin titer achieved in thermophilic P. thermoglucosidasius.
growth_records.notes: Genetic-tool / metabolic-engineering paper, NOT a GEM/MFA physiology paper. Only PRODUCT TITERS are reported for engineered riboflavin strains; no substrate uptake rate, no O2 rate, and no numerical specific growth rate (mu) are given, and no per-biomass (gDW) normalisation is possible because biomass at harvest is not reported. Growth was only 'monitored by OD600' (EnSpire plate reader) with no mu value stated. Riboflavin MW = 376.36 g/mol used for molar conversion. Derived volumetric rates: 5.03 g/L / 28 h = 0.1796 g/L/h; 5.03/376.36 = 13.36 mmol/L, /28 h = 0.477 mmol/L/h (these are VOLUMETRIC, not specific mmol/gDW/h, and are marked is_experimental:false because they are computed, not measured). Shake-flask endpoint titers for earlier strains (no fermentation time given, so no rate derivable): Gt-06 = 421 mg/L, Gt-08 = 521 mg/L, Gt-09 = 719 mg/L (all in 250 mL shake flasks, 250 rpm, 60 C). Fermentation medium composition is only in Supplementary Method 1 (not in the retrieved full text), so medium_composition_stated:false. Bioreactor temperature/pH/aeration not stated in full text (shake-flask fermentations were 60 C). Record retained for completeness of an engineered-prokaryote secretion observation; low confidence and NOT suitable as a quantitative GEM exchange-flux constraint.

growth_records.cite: Santana, Margarida M. et al. Search for the role of a Huc-type [NiFe]-hydrogenase of the soil thermophile Parageobacillus thermoglucosidasius. Frontiers in Microbiology (2026). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2026.1754804. [PMC12976888]
growth_records.snippet: grown in Nutrient Broth (BD Difco, Sparks, MD) at 60 C at different growth rates. Exponential growth under optimum conditions (2.2 h-1) was obtained in a bioreactor... Optimum doubling time 18 min, Growth rate 2.22 h-1 (Table 1)
growth_records.notes: Table 1 optimum condition. mu=2.22 h-1 reported (text rounds to 2.2). Doubling time reported 18 min = 0.30 h; cross-check mu=ln2/0.30=2.31 h-1 (consistent with 2.22 within rounding). No substrate uptake or product secretion rates measured (transcriptomics + qualitative whole-cell [NiFe]-hydrogenase activity study; hydrogenase activity reported only as dAbs/min/mg FW and dAbs/min/cfu, not mmol rates). T=60C. pH not stated.

growth_records.cite: Santana, Margarida M. et al. Search for the role of a Huc-type [NiFe]-hydrogenase of the soil thermophile Parageobacillus thermoglucosidasius. Frontiers in Microbiology (2026). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2026.1754804. [PMC12976888]
growth_records.snippet: Growth rates down to 0.025 h-1 were achieved in a chemostat... slow rate (chemostat; 1.1 days doubling time). Table 1: Slow growth doubling time 1.1 d, growth rate 0.025 h-1
growth_records.notes: Table 1 chemostat slow-growth condition. mu=D=0.025 h-1 reported. Doubling time reported 1.1 d = 26.4 h; cross-check mu=ln2/26.4=0.0263 h-1 (consistent). Nutrient Broth, 60C. No uptake/secretion rates measured. pH not stated.

growth_records.cite: Santana, Margarida M. et al. Search for the role of a Huc-type [NiFe]-hydrogenase of the soil thermophile Parageobacillus thermoglucosidasius. Frontiers in Microbiology (2026). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2026.1754804. [PMC12976888]
growth_records.snippet: Lower growth rates and near-zero growth rates were obtained in a retentostat... very slow and near-zero growth rates (retentostat; 14 d and 141 days doubling times). Table 1: Slow growth 14 d, growth rate 0.002 h-1
growth_records.notes: Table 1 retentostat very-slow condition. mu=0.002 h-1 reported. Doubling time reported 14 d = 336 h; cross-check mu=ln2/336=0.00206 h-1 (consistent). Retentostat: cells retained by tangential flow filtration, forced to share nutrient. Nutrient Broth, 60C. No uptake/secretion rates measured.

growth_records.cite: Santana, Margarida M. et al. Search for the role of a Huc-type [NiFe]-hydrogenase of the soil thermophile Parageobacillus thermoglucosidasius. Frontiers in Microbiology (2026). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2026.1754804. [PMC12976888]
growth_records.snippet: near-zero growth rates (retentostat; 14 d and 141 days doubling times). Table 1: Near-zero growth doubling time 141 d, growth rate 0.0002 h-1
growth_records.notes: Table 1 retentostat near-zero condition. mu=0.0002 h-1 reported. Doubling time reported 141 d = 3384 h; cross-check mu=ln2/3384=0.000205 h-1 (consistent, ~10,000-fold below optimum). Nutrient Broth, 60C. No uptake/secretion rates measured. This is the near-zero growth extreme of the retentostat series.

phenotypes.base_medium: Acid/gas-production test: peptone/complex base + substrate + pH indicator — positive = fermentative acid production, NOT growth on the substrate as a sole carbon source (interpret with care for model validation)
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 1430 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/1430
